title: Diporidium boivinianum
taxonomy: Diporidium boivinianum belongs to the family of flowering plants known as orchids. It is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae, which is a part of the Asparagaceae family. Further classification places it within the genus Diporidium. The species is considered to be a type of terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground. The exact classification of Diporidium boivinianum may vary depending on the source.

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as digestive issues and skin irritations.
